title: Rockstar Games confirms it was hacked by malicious group 'ShinyHunters' takes credit, gives until April 14 to pay ransom or it will release confidential data
summary: Rockstar Games has been breached by 'ShinyHunters', with hackers demanding ransom. The stolen data, including authentication tokens, does not include sensitive company or player information. The hackers have given Rockstar Games until April 14 to pay the ransom.
insight: Hacker group gains access to Rockstar's cloud infrastructure, putting sensitive data at risk.

title: Clippy, Microsoft s hapless Office assistant, was retired 25 years ago today its irritating spirit lives on in 100+ Copilots
summary: Microsoft's Clippy, a once-irritating Office assistant, was retired 25 years ago.
insight: Legacy of an AI can outlive its original purpose forever.

title: Two manufacturers commit to keep Blu-ray alive after others quit manufacturing Verbatim and I-O Data extend Blu-ray supply pledge as manufacturers exit the market
summary: Verbatim Japan and I-O Data have extended their commitment to supply Blu-ray products, including hardware and media, in Japan, amid declining demand and manufacturer exits.
insight: Blu-ray's future secured by remaining manufacturers in Japan.

title: MacBook user explains why he files the sharp metal edges off his Apple laptops unibody design facilitates a surprisingly deep roundover
summary: A MacBook user files off sharp edges to round over the unibody design, sparking controversy.
insight: Safety and comfort matter, even on high-end tech products like MacBooks.

title: Best Laptops 2026: Our benchmarked picks for productivity, portability, and battery life
summary: Our expert review features the top-rated laptops for productivity, portability, and battery life, covering various brands and operating systems. We've rigorously tested dozens of models to identify the best options. Our picks cater to diverse needs and preferences.
insight: The right laptop can boost work efficiency and personal productivity significantly.

title: Denuvo properly cracked in Resident Evil: Requiem, bypasses become plug-and-play cracked version runs faster, smoother, and uses way less VRAM and RAM
summary: A cracked version of Resident Evil: Requiem has been released, utilizing a properly cracked Denuvo anti-tamper system. The cracked version runs faster, smoother, and with reduced VRAM and RAM usage compared to the original game. This cracked version also features plug-and-play HV bypasses.
insight: Improved game performance without Denuvo's performance-harming restrictions

title: Intel Core Ultra 7 270K Plus vs Ryzen 7 9700X faceoff a new midrange CPU champ emerges
summary: Intel's Core Ultra 7 270K Plus takes on Ryzen 7 9700X in a mid-range showdown, pitting its $300 price point against the Ryzen's performance. Our testing pits the two CPUs against each other in various tasks to see which emerges victorious. The results highlight a competitive mid-range market.
insight: Mid-range CPU market sees intense competition between Intel and AMD.

title: HyperX Eve 1800 Review: Not worth $50
summary: The HyperX Eve 1800 keyboard falls short in features and usability, making it a poor value for its price. Despite its relatively low cost of $50, it lacks key performance and comfort features. Users may find alternative options more satisfying for the same price.
insight: Priced poorly for its lacking features and usability.

title: Chinese Nvidia Cloud Partner procured 300 servers with banned AI GPUs worth $92 million shares of data center supplier Sharetronic plummet following Super Micro smuggling arrest
summary: A Chinese AI data center company sold Nvidia H100-chipped servers despite US export ban, leading to a drop in shares of data center supplier Sharetronic after a Super Micro smuggling arrest.
insight: U.S. export ban circumvention undermines global AI technology security regulations.

title: Original Apollo 11 code open-sourced by NASA original Command Module and Lunar Module code repos are now public domain resources
summary: NASA has open-sourced the original Apollo 11 computer code, making it publicly accessible for anyone to read, download, and modify.
insight: Historic code preservation enables future space innovation and collaboration.

title: Benchmarking Nvidia's RTX Neural Texture Compression tech that can reduce VRAM usage by over 80%
summary: Nvidia's RTX Neural Texture Compression reduces VRAM usage by over 80% in various tests, showcasing its effectiveness in multiple GPU setups, including laptops.
insight: Reduced VRAM usage enables improved performance and increased graphical fidelity.

title: FAA courts gamers to become air traffic controllers boasts $155k average annual salary after three years as the agency prepares for 8,000 applicants
summary: The FAA is recruiting gamers to become air traffic controllers, offering a high average annual salary of $155k after three years. The agency aims to expand its numbers to address the ATC shortage, which affects safety and airport operations. This initiative targets the younger demographic to fill the shortage.
insight: Gamers' multitasking skills can help alleviate air traffic control shortage

title: Tests show $30,000 AI GPUs are terrible password crackers RTX 5090 gaming GPU outperforms Nvidia H200 and AMD MI300X
summary: A research team found the RTX 5090 gaming GPU outperforms AI datacenter GPUs in password cracking. This is significant because gaming GPUs are often cheaper and more accessible. The RTX 5090 was the winner in a benchmark test.
insight: Gaming GPUs beat AI datacenter GPUs in password cracking efficiency

title: $15 STM32U575 development board features FPC display connector, microSD card slot, two 48-pin GPIO headers
summary: The Maker Go STM32U575 development board features an STM32U575VGT6 MCU, 8MB of external flash, and a high-performance Cortex-M33 core running at 160 MHz. It has a display connector, a microSD card slot, and multiple GPIO headers for compact UI projects. The board is available for $15.
insight: Low-cost STM32U575 board simplifies compact UI development projects.

title: Anti-data center vote in Wisconsin puts future AI projects on notice
summary: Port Washington, Wisconsin, residents voted to require voter approval for data center tax incentives, potentially slowing down future development.
insight: Community control tightens around data center development and tax incentives.

title: Mozilla says Microsoft is using Copilot and Edge to tighten its grip on Windows
summary: Mozilla claims Microsoft's design choices in Windows favor Edge and Copilot, undermining user control.
insight: Microsoft's integration prioritizes its own products over competing software options.

title: Gmail encryption goes mobile, but email itself remains the weak link
summary: Google extends end-to-end encryption to Gmail mobile apps for users with organization-paid accounts, bolstering online security.
insight: Email remains a weak link in otherwise secure communication chains.

title: France starts moving government systems from Windows to Linux
summary: France's DINUM plans to move government systems from non-European software, including Windows, to Linux and European-developed apps.
insight: Enhancing national cybersecurity and reducing reliance on foreign technology

title: xAI sues Colorado over AI law, calling it a threat to free speech
summary: xAI is suing Colorado over an AI law it considers a threat to free speech. The dispute centers on control over information generated by large-scale AI systems. This frames the issue as a First Amendment matter.
insight: Free speech implications are at stake in AI regulation debates.
